Title: Reduced paucimannosidic N-glycan formation by suppression of a specific β-N-hexosaminidase from benthamiana

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Affects glycoprotein quality and heterogeneity in plant-made pharmaceuticals
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: trimming of terminal GlcNAc residues from complex N-glycans
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: complex N-glycans with terminal GlcNAc residues
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Removes terminal N-acetylglucosamine residues from complex N-glycans on glycoproteins, generating truncated paucimannosidic structures
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of paucimannosidic N-glycans; complements Arabidopsis hexo1 deficiency
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbHEXO1 was identified as a functional ortholog of AtHEXO1. 2. HEXO1 is glycosylated and localized to the vacuole. 3. Expression of HEXO1-GFP in Arabidopsis hexo1 mutants increased paucimannosidic MMXF and decreased complex GnGnXF glycans, demonstrating β-N-hexosaminidase activity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-PCR cloning; transient expression; subcellular localization by fluorescent tagging/confocal microscopy; Endo H/PNGase F digestion; complementation of Arabidopsis hexo1 mutant; MALDI-MS N-glycan profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves; overexpressed in Arabidopsis mutants
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Nicotiana benthamiana draft genome search; transcriptome database search
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Relevant for glycoengineering of plant production systems to manage paucimannosidic N-glycan formation, especially for vacuolar glycoproteins
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May increase undesired truncated glycans on recombinant products
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Considered functional ortholog of AtHEXO1; mainly relevant for vacuolar rather than secreted glycoproteins in this study.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Affects glycoprotein homogeneity and can alter therapeutic glycoprotein efficacy/engineering outcomes
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: secreted glycoproteins in apoplast/intercellular fluid; plasma membrane/apoplast-associated glycoproteins
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: trimming of terminal GlcNAc residues from secreted complex N-glycans
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: complex N-glycans on secreted recombinant glycoproteins carrying terminal GlcNAc residues
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cleaves terminal N-acetylglucosamine residues from complex N-glycans on secreted glycoproteins, generating truncated paucimannosidic glycans
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for paucimannosidic N-glycan formation on secreted recombinant glycoproteins
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of paucimannosidic N-glycans on secreted recombinant glycoproteins; RNAi suppression increases complex N-glycans on recombinant A1AT
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbHEXO3 was identified as a functional ortholog of AtHEXO3 and localized mainly to the plasma membrane/apoplast. 2. HEXO3 complements Arabidopsis hexo3 and hexo1 hexo3 mutants, restoring paucimannosidic MMXF glycan formation. 3. Overexpression of HEXO3 increased trimming of terminal GlcNAc from a secreted Fc reporter in wild-type plants. 4. Core α1,3-fucose enhanced HEXO3-dependent GlcNAc trimming, whereas β1,2-xylose and core α1,6-fucose did not. 5. RNAi knockdown of HEXO3 reduced paucimannosidic glycans and increased complex GnGn/GnGnXF glycans on recombinant human A1AT, demonstrating HEXO3 is a critical factor for unwanted truncation of secreted glycoprotein N-glycans.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-PCR cloning; transient expression; subcellular localization by fluorescent tagging/confocal microscopy; Endo H/PNGase F digestion; complementation of Arabidopsis hexo3 and hexo1 hexo3 mutants; co-expression assays with glycoprotein reporters; RNAi knockdown; MALDI-MS and LC-ESI-MS glycan profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves; overexpressed in Arabidopsis mutants; silenced by RNAi in Nicotiana benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Sec-Fc-mRFP substrate; A1AT substrate; FUT11; FUT8; XYLT
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Nicotiana benthamiana draft genome search; transcriptome database search
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient agroinfiltration in leaves; comparison of wild-type and ΔXT/FT glycoengineered plants
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for glycoengineering of Nicotiana benthamiana to suppress unwanted paucimannosidic glycan formation on therapeutic glycoproteins; RNAi and future genome editing/knockout proposed; conversely, overexpression can be used to generate mannose-terminated glycans for enzymes such as glucocerebrosidase
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression increases undesirable truncated glycans and product heterogeneity for many therapeutics; transient RNAi gave incomplete suppression leaving residual paucimannosidic glycans
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Core α1,3-fucose specifically enhances HEXO3-mediated trimming; authors propose complete knockout to determine whether additional HEXO candidates also contribute.
